biological_activities.mic_unit: mµmoles per mg (dry weight) of cells

mechanism_of_action: Antimycin A inhibits metabolic reduction of cytochrome c and interferes with electron transfer between cytochromes b and c. It hinders plasma-membrane functions by inhibiting concentrative uptake of α-aminoisobutyrate and α-methylglucoside, leading to extensive loss of inorganic phosphate and other cellular substances. At higher concentrations, it inhibits aerobic respiration and acts as a lytic agent for isolated protoplasts. Its lytic mechanism involves association with the lipid-rich plasma membrane, causing lesions and outflow of small molecules, akin to detergent-induced lysis.

toxicity_profile.cytotoxicity: Antimycin A is a powerful respiratory inhibitor for mammalian cells.
